markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's confirmed November 19, 2026 launch date for Grand Theft Auto VI has locked in a tight five-month window that now shapes trader views on preceding cultural milestones. Take-Two's recent earnings call tied record fiscal projections directly to the release, reinforcing the timeline after prior delays and prompting other studios to shift fall 2026 schedules around the blockbuster. This anticipation fuels markets on music drops such as Playboi Carti's next album or Drake's Iceman project, alongside high-probability geopolitical resolutions like a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire that could occur in the interim. Traders weigh streaming metrics, artist release strategies, and verified announcements against the fixed GTA VI date, recognizing that chart dominance, viral campaigns, and major news cycles often accelerate or stall in the shadow of Rockstar's marketing push. Upcoming earnings updates and any surprise trailer or pre-order reveals could further shift momentum before the game arrives.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Recent mistrials and repeated prosecutorial setbacks in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials have driven trader consensus toward “No Prison Time” at 81.9% implied probability.** The former producer’s third Manhattan trial on the Jessica Mann rape allegation ended in a hung jury and mistrial on May 15, 2026—the second such deadlock in roughly a year—leaving the Manhattan DA with a late-June deadline to decide whether to pursue a fourth trial. 

Weinstein, now 74 and in frail health with reported cancer, diabetes, and recent heart issues, has already served more than five years since his 2020 arrest while his separate 16-year California conviction remains under appeal and consecutive to any New York sentence. Traders appear to price in a strong likelihood of plea negotiations, time-served resolutions, dropped charges, or successful appeals given the prosecution’s track record of failing to secure unanimous verdicts and the practical challenges of retrying a high-profile, aging defendant at Rikers. 

Upcoming catalysts center on the June 24 hearing and any rulings on the California appeal that could further limit additional exposure.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold an 80.5% implied probability as 2026 Stanley Cup champions, driven by their 3-2 series lead over the Vegas Golden Knights after a 4-2 Game 5 victory on June 11 that featured two power-play goals. The Hurricanes' home-ice advantage for a potential Game 7 in Raleigh, combined with strong regular-season form (53-22-7) and consistent playoff depth, underpins trader consensus. Vegas, at 19.9%, advanced as Western Conference underdogs with resilient performances in earlier rounds but now faces elimination pressure following alternating results in a high-scoring Final. Recent momentum shifts, including Carolina's road and home wins, have widened the gap in market pricing.

markets.question: Will the Vegas Golden Knights win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Vegas Golden Knights win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold an 80.5% implied probability as 2026 Stanley Cup champions, driven by their 3-2 series lead over the Vegas Golden Knights after a 4-2 Game 5 victory on June 11 that featured two power-play goals. The Hurricanes' home-ice advantage for a potential Game 7 in Raleigh, combined with strong regular-season form (53-22-7) and consistent playoff depth, underpins trader consensus. Vegas, at 19.9%, advanced as Western Conference underdogs with resilient performances in earlier rounds but now faces elimination pressure following alternating results in a high-scoring Final. Recent momentum shifts, including Carolina's road and home wins, have widened the gap in market pricing.

markets.question: Will the New York Knicks win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the New York Knicks win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 80% implied probability of capturing the 2026 NBA title after seizing a 3-1 lead in the Finals against the San Antonio Spurs. Their commanding position stems from a record-setting 29-point comeback victory in Game 4, capped by OG Anunoby’s tip-in with 1.2 seconds left, extending their playoff win streak and placing them one victory from ending a 53-year championship drought. The Spurs, despite a strong regular-season record and Victor Wembanyama’s presence, trail after dropping the first two games on the road and failing to close out Game 4 at home. Historical data shows teams trailing 3-1 in the Finals recover only rarely, aligning with current trader consensus reflected in the pricing. Game 5 is scheduled for June 13 in San Antonio.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: MegaETH airdrop by...?
events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain edges France as the narrow market leader among a tightly clustered group of contenders, reflecting their Euro 2024 title, recent UEFA Nations League success, and a maturing squad featuring standout talents. France counters with proven final appearances and exceptional depth, while Portugal, England, Argentina, and Brazil occupy similar implied probabilities thanks to established stars, strong qualifying campaigns, and historical pedigree. The expanded 48-team format, recent form in continental competitions, and questions around key player availability have kept probabilities bunched, underscoring the absence of a dominant side entering the group stage.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's continued leadership of the Chinese Communist Party, including his roles as general secretary and Central Military Commission chairman, underpins trader expectations that he will remain in power through 2026. Recent public activities, such as hosting a U.S. presidential visit, outlining priorities for the 15th Five-Year Plan, and addressing party forums, demonstrate sustained authority without signs of internal challenge or health-related withdrawal. Military purges and institutional adjustments have reinforced centralized control rather than eroding it, while the absence of a designated successor ahead of the 21st Party Congress in 2027 aligns with patterns of extended tenure. Speculation from mid-2025 about potential instability has not materialized into verifiable developments that would shift the consensus reflected in current pricing.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination amid a fragmented field, reflecting his national profile as California governor, fundraising strength, and media appearances positioning him against the current administration. Polling averages show a fluid race where Buttigieg, Harris, and Shapiro draw support from different voter blocs, while AOC consolidates progressive preferences. Key differentiators include executive records among governors versus Senate or House visibility, swing-state appeal, and ideological positioning as Democrats assess paths to broaden coalitions ahead of the midterms. No formal announcements have occurred, leaving room for late entrants or shifts driven by fundraising, primary debates, or 2026 results to consolidate support.
